   <item>
      <title>Doc Rivers, Bucks Parting Ways</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285074/Doc-Rivers-Bucks-Parting-Ways</link>
      <description><p>Doc Rivers is departing the Milwaukee Bucks as head coach. Rivers is stepping down after a 32-50 season. Milwaukee will be looking for their third head coach in the last three years.</p>
<p>In two-plus seasons on the Bucks sidelines, Rivers went 97-103. That's the Hall of Famer's only losing record over the five franchises he's coached.</p>
<p>Rivers led Milwaukee to the playoffs twice, but the Bucks exited in the first round each time. This season, the team didn't even advance to the Play-In Tournament.</p>
<p>The Bucks will reportedly pay out the final season on Rivers' deal in 2026-27. A new head coach will join the franchise in a critical juncture, as Giannis Antetokounmpo's future in Milwaukee remains in doubt.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 12 Apr 2026 20:54:20 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285074/Doc-Rivers-Bucks-Parting-Ways</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Rivers_Doc_mil_240226.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Billy Donovan Plans Meeting With Bulls' Ownership To Discuss Future</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285075/Billy-Donovan-Plans-Meeting-With-Bulls-Ownership-To-Discuss-Future</link>
      <description><p>Billy Donovan expressed uncertainty Sunday about his future with the Chicago Bulls, stating the organization has reached a "pivot point" following the dismissal of its top two basketball operations executives last week.</p>
<p>Donovan said he plans to meet with team president and CEO Michael Reinsdorf in the coming days to determine the path forward.</p>
<p>"I love being in Chicago and the organization and everything about it," Donovan said before the team's regular-season finale at the Dallas Mavericks. "But we're at this pivot point right now where everything has changed. Find out where we're all at on this. And how we're going to move forward."</p>
<p>The Bulls last week dismissed executive vice president of basketball operations Arturas Karnisovas and general manager Marc Eversley. At a subsequent news conference, Reinsdorf voiced support for Donovan's continued role as head coach.</p>
<p>Reinsdorf previously stated that any front office candidate uninterested in retaining Donovan would likely not be the right fit. He later clarified in writing, however, that Donovan's status would not take "precedence in the process."</p>
<p>Donovan said outside opportunities would not factor into his decision, noting he had declined to pursue other openings in recent years out of loyalty to Chicago.</p>
<p>"I'm committed here to Chicago," Donovan said. "I'm going to try to be where my feet are planted. I'm going to stay there and stay committed to where I'm at."</p>
<p>Donovan holds a 226-255 record across six seasons in Chicago. He had been considered for the vacant head coaching position at the University of North Carolina before the school hired Michael Malone.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 12 Apr 2026 21:22:49 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285075/Billy-Donovan-Plans-Meeting-With-Bulls-Ownership-To-Discuss-Future</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Donovan_Billy_chi_240410.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Patrick Dumont Optimistic About Mavs' Future With 'Generational Talent' Cooper Flagg</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285078/Patrick-Dumont-Optimistic-About-Mavs-Future-With-Generational-Talent-Cooper-Flagg</link>
      <description><p>Dallas Mavericks governor Patrick Dumont expressed confidence Sunday that 19-year-old Cooper Flagg gives the franchise a foundation to compete for championships, even as the franchise closes the season firmly in the lottery and faces critical decisions on basketball leadership and a new arena.</p>
<p>"Having a generational talent like Cooper Flagg should give everyone confidence, our fans, our team, that we're going to get back to the playoffs and that we're going to try to compete for championships as soon as we can," Dumont told the Dallas Morning News.</p>
<p>Dumont announced the franchise will begin an aggressive search for a new president of basketball operations Monday, targeting a mid-May hire. The position has been vacant since Nico Harrison's dismissal on November 11 following a 3-8 season start. Acting co-general managers Matt Riccardi and Michael Finley have managed basketball operations since and are considered internal candidates.</p>
<p>"Monday morning, we're going to get active, and we're going to talk to a lot of people," Dumont said. "Our goal is to have someone on board by the middle of May."</p>
<p>Dumont outlined his priorities for the role, stressing culture and communication alongside basketball acumen.</p>
<p>"They're going to set the tone for everything that we do from a basketball perspective," he said. "It's got to be a culture that will lead us to win championships and someone that can build that culture."</p>
<p>Sports Illustrated reported Dumont is targeting high-profile candidates including Oklahoma City Thunder vice president Sam Presti and Boston Celtics president Brad Stevens. Dumont declined to confirm names, citing league tampering rules.</p>
<p>"I can't talk to any of those people now because it would be tampering. So anything that you may have heard is just rumor and chatter," he said. "We'll figure out who's a great leader. Who has a track record. Who knows how to work with young players and develop them."</p>
<p>The presidential search runs parallel to a July 1 deadline to select a site for a new basketball-specific arena and 50-acre entertainment district. Dallas has narrowed options to the former Valley View Mall tract in North Dallas and a downtown location. Dumont cited Golden State's $1.4 billion Chase Center as the benchmark.</p>
<p>"I think Chase Center is definitely the leading benchmark, just because of the experience it provides to fans," he said. "The in-game experience, the viewing angles from seats. The amenities that it has."</p>
<p>On the February 2025 Luka Doncic trade and the franchise's difficult year, Dumont accepted responsibility.</p>
<p>"When expectations aren't met, we really have to take a critical look at everything we're doing," he said. "I really believe this is an extremely important offseason for this franchise. We're going to work tirelessly to get things right."</p>
<p>Minority owner Mark Cuban said in a late-March podcast that he regrets selling to the Adelson-Dumont families. Dumont responded diplomatically.</p>
<p>"Mark is really passionate about the Mavericks, and I think that's great," Dumont said. "I think he's really, really frustrated about the year that the team has had. And I understand that frustration.</p>
<p>"I consider Mark a friend. And look, I always appreciate his thoughts. And I appreciate his frustration.  </p>
<p>"But one thing I do want to say, in terms of ownership, I think we've been really clear: We're not moving the team to another city. The team is not for sale. We're going to continue to invest in this franchise. We're going to build a new home for the team, and hopefully in the long term we'll be able to celebrate that success for many years."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 12 Apr 2026 22:00:44 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285078/Patrick-Dumont-Optimistic-About-Mavs-Future-With-Generational-Talent-Cooper-Flagg</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Dumont_Patrick_dal_250210.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Kings To Retain Doug Christie As Head Coach Next Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285062/Kings-To-Retain-Doug-Christie-As-Head-Coach-Next-Season</link>
      <description><p>The Sacramento Kings will bring back head coach Doug Christie next season despite finishing at the bottom of the Western Conference standings, team sources told ESPN on Sunday.</p>
<p>General manager Scott Perry and Kings leadership believe Christie never had a fair opportunity with a healthy roster. Sacramento's projected starting lineup never played a single minute together this season.</p>
<p>Christie took over as interim coach in December 2024 after the Kings dismissed Mike Brown. Perry signed Christie to a three-year deal last summer after assuming control of franchise decision-making.</p>
<p>Starting wing Keegan Murray suffered a torn thumb ligament in the preseason, and center Domantas Sabonis went down in November with a partially torn meniscus, limiting him to 19 games before season-ending surgery. Veteran guard Zach LaVine played just 39 games before requiring surgery on his right hand.</p>
<p>Sacramento stumbled to a 3-13 start before finishing 12-46 at one point. The franchise has since pivoted toward a youth movement.</p>
<p>Part of the retention decision reflects positive signs from rookie big men Maxime Raynaud and Dylan Cardwell, as well as first-round forward Nique Clifford.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 12 Apr 2026 12:12:28 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285062/Kings-To-Retain-Doug-Christie-As-Head-Coach-Next-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Christie_Doug_sac_241227.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Nick Smith Jr, Lakers Signing Two-Year Contact</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285061/Nick-Smith-Jr-Lakers-Signing-Two-Year-Contact</link>
      <description><p>The Los Angeles Lakers are signing guard Nick Smith Jr. to a two-year standard contract. Smith will fill the Lakers 15th standard roster spot that was opened when the team waived guard Kobe Bufkin. Smith will be eligible to play in the playoffs for the Lakers.</p>
<p>In 29 NBA games for Los Angeles, the 6-foot-2 guard has averaged 6.0 points in 12.3 minutes per game. Smith shot 40% on 2.8 three-point attempts per game with the Lakers.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Flory Bidunga, Jackson Shelstad Transferring To Louisville</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285073/Flory-Bidunga-Jackson-Shelstad-Transferring-To-Louisville</link>
      <description><p>Louisville has received commitments from big man Flory Bidunga and guard Jackson Shelstad to transfer for the 2026-27 college basketball season. Bidunga played for Kansas for the last two seasons. Shelstad has spent the last three seasons with Oregon.</p>
<p>Bidunga started in 34 of 35 games for the Jayhawks as a sophomore. The 6-foot-10 center averaged 13.3 points, 9.0 rebounds and 2.6 blocks per game last season.</p>
<p>Due to a hand injury, Shelstad played in only 12 games for the Ducks as a junior. Overall, Shelstad started in 77 of 79 games. The 6-foot guard averaged 13.6 points, 4.9 assists and a steal per game during his Oregon career.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 12 Apr 2026 17:34:25 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285073/Flory-Bidunga-Jackson-Shelstad-Transferring-To-Louisville</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_Louisville.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Caleb Holt Leads USA Past Team World 102-100 In OT Thriller At Nike Hoop Summit</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285055/Caleb-Holt-Leads-USA-Past-Team-World-102-100-In-OT-Thriller-At-Nike-Hoop-Summit</link>
      <description><p>Team USA survived a determined effort from Team World to claim a 102-100 overtime victory Saturday at the 2026 Nike Hoop Summit. The narrow win required an extra period to separate two evenly matched rosters packed with elite prospects that will prominently feature in the 2027 NBA draft.</p>
<p>Caleb Holt was the standout performer for Team USA as the Arizona commit posted 24 points, 8 rebounds and 7 steals on 9-of-18 shooting, including 4-of-9 from three-point range, in 33 minutes. Holt was previously named MVP of the McDonald's All-American game. Jason Crowe Jr., the Missouri-bound guard out of Inglewood High School, added 16 points, while Tyran Stokes contributed 15.</p>
<p>Team World was led by Miles Sadler, the 5-foot-11 West Virginia-bound point guard from Brampton, Ontario, who delivered a game-high 29 points, 7 assists and 2 steals on 10-of-18 shooting across 43 minutes. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/highschool/nike_hoop_summit/stats/Historical">Sadler's 29 points are the eighth most in Hoop Summit history</a>.</p>
<p>Abdou Toure, the Arkansas-bound forward representing Guinea and the United States, posted 19 points and 6 rebounds with 5 blocks. Paul Osaruyi added 11 points and 5 rebounds with a team-best +19 rating.</p>
<p>Brandon McCoy Jr. provided a steady two-way presence for Team USA with 11 points, 5 assists, 4 steals and 3 blocks. Christian Collins, uncommitted out of St. John Bosco High School, contributed 7 rebounds and a team-high +21 rating off the bench in 17 minutes.</p>
<p>For Team World, Finland's Miikka Muurinen recorded 10 points and 8 rebounds in 15 minutes despite a team-worst -21 rating. Nigeria's Ikenna Alozie, the Houston-bound guard, posted 6 points, 7 rebounds and 4 steals. Lucas Morillo of the Dominican Republic, committed to Illinois, chipped in 12 points and 3 steals.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/highschool/nike_hoop_summit/home">Team USA has now won six straight Hoop Summit competitions</a>. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 12 Apr 2026 07:50:39 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285055/Caleb-Holt-Leads-USA-Past-Team-World-102-100-In-OT-Thriller-At-Nike-Hoop-Summit</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_usa.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Wembanyama, Giannis Among Surge Of NBA Players Citing Chess As Favorite Mental Training Tool</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285056/Wembanyama-Giannis-Among-Surge-Of-NBA-Players-Citing-Chess-As-Favorite-Mental-Training-Tool</link>
      <description><p>Chess has quietly taken hold across NBA locker rooms, with some of the game's biggest stars crediting the centuries-old board game for sharpening their basketball instincts. The pattern recognition, anticipation, and strategic thinking developed playing chess translates directly to their on-court play.</p>
<p>For Giannis Antetokounmpo, he learned chess at age ten at a church in Greece. When he stripped LeBron James from behind with ten seconds remaining to seal a Milwaukee Bucks win in January, he cited preparation more than improvisation.</p>
<p>"I knew down the stretch that LeBron gets the ball. I knew that he wanted to get downhill and make the play. I knew that he's going to call the pick-and-roll," Antetokounmpo told ESPN as part of its feature on chess. "I've played against him a lot of times, so I was just thinking ahead. Just to position myself to be more successful at the play."</p>
<p>The game's influence also surfaced in one of the most memorable plays in NBA Finals history, his block on Deandre Ayton in Game 4 of the series. </p>
<p>Victor Wembanyama has taken chess devotion further than almost anyone. A frequent offseason workout involves conditioning and playing chess simultaneously, a deliberate attempt to replicate the mental fatigue of late-game decision-making.</p>
<p>"I figure it's like hitting two birds with one stone. Sometimes you just need to get away," Wembanyama told ESPN. "You don't have the focus needed to read or the focus needed to study something, so chess is good in that sense."</p>
<p>Rudy Gobert faces Wembanyama in chess "almost every time" their teams meet and understands the conditioning drill's purpose.</p>
<p>"When you're just sitting and you're just playing chess, you don't have to do anything else but focus on the board," Gobert told ESPN. "But when you actually start to get some fatigue, mental fatigue, physical fatigue, I think it's harder to make right decisions, so it kind of challenges you even more."</p>
<p>In December 2024, Wembanyama infamously invited fans to play him at Washington Square Park in New York City, going 2-2 in four public matches. He later advocated for "an NBA players-only chess tournament." Months later, Derrick Rose delivered exactly that with his Chesstival event in Las Vegas.</p>
<p>For Rose, chess is inseparable from his identity as a point guard. He learned the game more than a decade ago after reading about its potential to prevent Alzheimer's and dementia, eventually carrying a board during the final five seasons of his playing career.</p>
<p>"It was more of, I'm a point guard. Seeing two or three plays down the line or being able to have that poise when you're getting your ass kicked," Rose told ESPN. "So being able to fight through that."</p>
<p>Rose played chess as part of his pregame routine, listening to Bob Marley and moving pieces until he stepped onto the court. </p>
<p>"That one move, you have to think before you move that next piece, that it may f--- up the whole game. Even though it may seem so minute at that time, but it may mess up your guard," Rose said.</p>
<p>Rajon Rondo, now a Bucks associate coach, used chess as direct mental preparation during his playing days with the Clippers, facing teammate Ivica Zubac before every game.</p>
<p>"That kind of just allowed me to get my thinking cap on a little bit earlier," Rondo said. "And obviously thinking in strategy to start the game."</p>
<p>Rondo also draws a direct line between chess study and film study, two disciplines built on reading opponents.</p>
<p>"Film and studying are always great habits to pick up and that's just like in chess," Rondo said. "Only way you get better is watching your games and try to watch those back and figure out the opponent's moves."</p>
<p>Beyond the competitive benefits, chess has become a genuine off-court pastime, a mental alternative to phone scrolling during long road trips and a way for players to track measurable personal growth through their rating systems. Rose's Chesstival drew Rondo, Grant Williams, Quinten Post, Brandon Ingram, Onyeka Okongwu and others, with Post defeating Ingram in the finals.</p>
<p>Rose's ambitions extend further as he plans a future Chesstival at Madison Square Garden focused on kids and women, while Wembanyama hosted a community chess event in his hometown of Le Chesney, France, last summer. Rondo intends to introduce mandatory chess tournaments at his AAU program in place of video games during travel.</p>
<p>"I want to change the AAU grassroots program," Rondo said.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 12 Apr 2026 08:07:15 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285056/Wembanyama-Giannis-Among-Surge-Of-NBA-Players-Citing-Chess-As-Favorite-Mental-Training-Tool</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wembanyama_Victor_sas_231028.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Tre Scott, Nets Sign Contract</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285080/Tre-Scott-Nets-Sign-Contract</link>
      <description><p>The Brooklyn Nets have signed forward Trevon Scott to a contract.</p>
<p>Scott, 6-foot-8 and 225 pounds, originally joined Brooklyn on a 10-day hardship contract April 2. Scott averaged 8.0 points, 5.6 rebounds, 1.8 assists, and 1.4 steals in 31.2 minutes across five appearances.</p>
<p>The 26-year-old posted back-to-back career-high scoring efforts of 11 points against Indiana on April 9 and Milwaukee on April 10. He also set a personal best with four steals against the Bucks on April 7.</p>
<p>Scott spent the bulk of this season with Brooklyn's G League affiliate, the Long Island Nets, appearing in 47 total games and averaging 12.0 points, 4.7 rebounds, 2.0 assists, and 1.1 steals in 27.9 minutes per outing.</p>
<p>Across 199 career G League games with six affiliates, Scott has averaged 10.7 points, 5.6 rebounds, 1.8 assists, and 1.1 steals. His stops include the Salt Lake City Stars, Cleveland Charge, Agua Caliente Clippers, Greensboro Swarm, and Osceola Magic before landing with Long Island.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 12 Apr 2026 22:42:58 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285080/Tre-Scott-Nets-Sign-Contract</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_brooklynnets.jpg" />
   </item>

      <description><p>The Eastern Conference postseason picture is set. The 10 postseason teams are:</p>
<ol>
<li style="text-align: left;">Detroit Pistons</li>
<li style="text-align: left;">Boston Celtics</li>
<li style="text-align: left;">New York Knicks</li>
<li style="text-align: left;">Cleveland Cavaliers</li>
<li style="text-align: left;">Toronto Raptors</li>
<li style="text-align: left;">Atlanta Hawks</li>
<li style="text-align: left;">Philadelphia 76ers</li>
<li style="text-align: left;">Orlando Magic</li>
<li style="text-align: left;">Charlotte Hornets</li>
<li style="text-align: left;">Miami Heat</li>
</ol>
<p>The 76ers will host the Magic in the 7-8 Play-In Tournament Game. The winner will advance to the Playoffs to play the Celtics as the #7 seed. The loser will play whoever wins when the Hornets host the Heat in the 9-10 Play-In Tournament Game. The winner of that game will advance to the Playoffs to play the Pistons as the #8 seed.</p>
<p>The other first-round series will feature the Knicks hosting the Hawks and the Cavaliers hosting the Raptors.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>How To Watch Kings Vs Trail Blazers, Live Stream, TV Channel, Predictions</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285072/How-To-Watch-Kings-Vs-Trail-Blazers-Live-Stream-TV-Channel-Predictions</link>
      <description><p style="position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; text-align: center; z-index: 9999; margin: 0; padding: 10px; background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9);"><a href="https://www.fubo.tv/stream/nba/?irad=1846663&irmp=3589123&subId1=RealGM" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><input style="background-color: #ea7910; color: #ffffff; border-color: #141414; border-radius: 65px; cursor: pointer; border-width: 3px; padding: 10px; margin: 0;" type="button" value="STREAM KINGS VS TRAIL BLAZERS ON FUBO TODAY!" /> </a></p>
<p style="position: fixed; top: 0px; left: 0px; width: 100%; text-align: center; z-index: 9999; margin: 0px; padding-top: 10px; padding-right: 10px; padding-bottom: 10px; background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9);"> </p>
<p>The 2025-26 NBA regular season reaches its final day this Sunday, April 12, as the <strong>Portland Trail Blazers</strong> host the <strong>Sacramento Kings</strong> at the Moda Center. For Portland (41-40), the stakes couldn't be higher: a victory tonight officially clinches the No. 8 seed in the Western Conference, granting them two chances to secure a playoff spot via the Play-In tournament.</p>
<p><a href="https://www.fubo.tv/stream/nba/?irad=1846663&irmp=3589123&subId1=RealGM" target="_blank" rel="noopener"> <input style="background-color: #ea7910; color: #ffffff; border-color: #141414; border-radius: 65px; cursor: pointer; border-width: 3px; padding: 10px; margin: 10px;" type="button" value="WATCH THE KINGS VS TRAIL BLAZERS" /> </a></p>
<div id="fuboWidgetContain" style="max-width: 640px;">
<div class="fuboWidget" style="position: relative; padding-top: 66.05%;"><iframe class="video" style="position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%;" src="https://www.fubo.tv/stream/nba/?irad=1846663&irmp=3589123&subId1=RealGM" frameborder="0" scrolling="no" allowfullscreen="allowfullscreen"></iframe></div>
</div>
<p>The Sacramento Kings (22-59) arrive in Portland having endured a difficult campaign marred by season-ending injuries to stars like <strong>Domantas Sabonis</strong> and <strong>Zach LaVine</strong>. Despite their record, Sacramento has played spoiler recently, picking up wins against Golden State and New Orleans this month. However, they face a Trail Blazers squad that has won three straight at home and is led by the elite scoring of All-Star forward <strong>Deni Avdija</strong>. Catch the finale live on Fubo. </p>
<p>The "urgency factor" is the defining theme of this matchup. While the Kings are focused on their 2026 draft lottery positioning, the Trail Blazers are treating this as a pre-playoff elimination game. Portland has dominated the season series 3-0, including a wild 134-133 overtime victory in December.    <item>
      <title>Nuggets To Sit Most Of Rotation Sunday; Nikola Jokic Questionable With Award Eligibility At Stake</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285054/Nuggets-To-Sit-Most-Of-Rotation-Sunday;-Nikola-Jokic-Questionable-With-Award-Eligibility-At-Stake</link>
      <description><p>The Denver Nuggets again ruled out most of their regular rotation Sunday against the San Antonio Spurs, but three-time MVP Nikola Jokic remains questionable. Jokic needs at least 15 minutes to secure eligibility for postseason awards.</p>
<p>Denver enters Sunday as the No. 3 seed in the Western Conference at 53-28. A loss, combined with a Los Angeles Lakers win over the Utah Jazz, would drop the Nuggets to the No. 4 seed. The Lakers hold the tiebreaker over Denver following an overtime victory last month.</p>
<p>The seeding difference carries significant playoff implications. As the No. 3 seed, Denver would face the Minnesota Timberwolves in the first round. A slide to No. 4 would instead send them against the Houston Rockets.</p>
<p>The Nuggets will be without Jamal Murray, Aaron Gordon, Cameron Johnson, Christian Braun and Tim Hardaway Jr. on Sunday. Peyton Watson and Spencer Jones remain sidelined as well with right hamstring strains.</p>
<p>The Spurs listed Victor Wembanyama, Stephon Castle and Devin Vassell as questionable. Wembanyama already secured his award eligibility by playing Friday. The Spurs are locked into the No. 2 seed but could influence Denver's bracket placement with a victory.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 11 Apr 2026 22:48:32 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285054/Nuggets-To-Sit-Most-Of-Rotation-Sunday;-Nikola-Jokic-Questionable-With-Award-Eligibility-At-Stake</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Jokic_Nikola_den_250130.jpg" />
   </item>

      <description><p>Illinois guard Keaton Wagler has declared as an Early Entry Candidate for the 2026 NBA Draft. Wagler is currently ranked fifth on ESPN's Top 100 prospects for this year's draft.</p>

      <title>Raptors Convert AJ Lawson To Standard Contract</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285052/Raptors-Convert-AJ-Lawson-To-Standard-Contract</link>
      <description><p>The Toronto Raptors are converting wing A.J. Lawson from his two-way contract to a standard contract for the remainder of the season. This will make Lawson eligible for the postseason for Toronto.</p>
<p>Lawson has appeared in 23 games for Toronto this season. The 6-foot-6 wing has averaged 4.0 points in 9.4 minutes per game for the Raptors. Lawson has shot 41.5% from behind the arc in his limited minutes in the NBA this season.</p>
<p>Toronto now has a full roster as they head into the postseason. If the Raptors win on Sunday, they'll secure a top-six seed and a playoff spot. It Toronto loses, they could finish anywhere from sixth to eighth place in the Eastern Conference, depending on the results of other games.</p></description>

      <description><p>Michigan coach Dusty May has agreed on a deal that will have him continuing to lead the Wolverines "for many years to come," athletic director Warde Manuel said Saturday.</p>
<p>Manuel made the comment during a ceremony celebrating Michigan's NCAA tournament championship.</p>
<p>"Dusty and I have already reached an agreement," Manuel said in a remark that resulted in a standing ovation as well as chants of "Dusty!" from the crowd that had gathered at the Crisler Center.</p>
<p>Manuel then finished his comment: "And he will be the leader of this basketball team for many years to come."</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Stephen Curry Tweaks Ankle In Loss To Kings, Expects To Play Sunday</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285050/Stephen-Curry-Tweaks-Ankle-In-Loss-To-Kings-Expects-To-Play-Sunday</link>
      <description><p>Stephen Curry suffered an ankle scare Friday during the Golden State Warriors' 124-118 loss to the Sacramento Kings, but expects to be healthy for Wednesday's 9/10 Play-In game, which will likely be against the Los Angeles Clippers. The 38-year-old guard slipped on a wet spot on the floor with just over nine minutes remaining in the first quarter, coming up hobbling and sending concern through the building.</p>
<p>Steve Kerr immediately called timeout and watched Curry consult with Warriors VP of player health and performance Rick Celebrini on the bench. Curry returned to the game shortly after.</p>
<p>"He's doing fine," Kerr said. "It was his ankle, it wasn't his knee. That's why I took the timeout. I was worried it was his knee. I just saw him limping and he came over and said, 'No, I'm fine. It's just the ankle.' He just tweaked it or something but he's fine."</p>
<p>Curry finished with 11 points on 3-of-8 shooting in 27 minutes, appearing uncomfortable through much of the first half before finding better rhythm after the break. He spent time with his ankle in an ice bucket postgame and received additional treatment from the training staff.</p>
<p>The context makes the injury particularly nerve-wracking. Curry is playing in just his third game back after missing more than two months with a runner's knee injury.</p>
<p>"As long as it's not my knee, I can deal with ankles," Curry said. "My knee felt pretty good, even better than last game, so I'll take that as a positive."</p>
<p>Kerr confirmed the plan is for Curry to play Sunday's regular-season finale against the Clippers. Curry said he is targeting 30-32 minutes to build rhythm ahead of Wednesday's winner-take-all play-in matchup.</p>
<p>"I'll be comfortable with it," Curry said. "I find it hard to believe that if the game calls for a couple extra minutes that we won't make the right decision there."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 11 Apr 2026 14:20:18 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285050/Stephen-Curry-Tweaks-Ankle-In-Loss-To-Kings-Expects-To-Play-Sunday</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Curry_Stephen_gsw_241227.jpg" />
   </item>
